name: Use of Path Manipulation Function without Maximum-sized Buffer
description: The product calls a path or filename normalization function (such as realpath or PathAppend) but provides an output buffer smaller than the maximum possible size (e.g., PATH_MAX). This can result in a buffer overflow when the resolved path exceeds the buffer size.
detection_pattern: INGRESS node provides path data to a TRANSFORM node (path normalization function) that writes to a STORAGE node (buffer) without a CONTROL node verifying the buffer is sized to PATH_MAX or the maximum possible output length.
